summary: The agent correctly identifies that abnormal breathing can introduce variability into measurements; thus, advising patients with cardiac tamponade to breathe normally helps ensure accurate and stable blood pressure readings without compromising physiological integrity. This approach supports reliable hemodynamics evaluation while avoiding unnecessary interventions based on unsupported assumptions about respiratory effects.
